title: Step Forward Fund 2026: National Lottery Grants for Grassroots Organizations in England
summary: The National Lottery Community Fund has officially opened applications for The Step Forward Fund, a groundbreaking and equity-driven grant programme designed specifically to support grassroots organizations led by and serving Global Majority and racially minoritised communities across England. With a total pot of 4 million pounds available and individual awards ranging from 100,000 to 225,000 pounds per organization, this fund represents one of the most significant targeted investments in community-led organizations from racially minoritised backgrounds that England has seen in recent years. This article provides a comprehensive, in-depth guide to The Step Forward Fund what it is, who it is for, how much funding is available, what the money can be used for, how to apply, and what to expect throughout the process. What Is The Step Forward Fund? The Step Forward Fund is a National Lottery Community Fund grant programme that has been co-designed in partnership with The Phoenix Community Trust and their partners. It was developed collaboratively with and for Global Majority and racially minoritised communities, ensuring that the language, design, and priorities of the fund are grounded in the authentic experiences and self-determined needs of the communities it seeks to serve. T he fund has been created to address a specific and well-documented challenge: many grassroots organizations led by Global Majority and racially minoritised communities have successfully received smaller project-based grants, but struggle to make the leap to larger, more sustained investment that would allow them to grow, develop their organizational infrastructure, and deliver long-term impact. The Step Forward Fund is designed to bridge exactly that gap. It provides multi-year funding to help organizations: Continue to advocate for and care for their communities Develop and grow their organizational capacity Sustain and build on the positive impact of their work This fund is part of The National Lottery Community Fund s broader commitment to equity in grant-making specifically its commitment to investing life-changing funding where there is greatest need and putting community power at the heart of how grants are designed and awarded. The National Lottery Community Fund s Commitment to Equity The Step Forward Fund does not exist in isolation. It is one expression of a much deeper institutional commitment from The National Lottery Community Fund to address systemic inequity in the funding landscape. For decades, research and lived experience have consistently shown that grassroots organizations led by Black, Asian, and racially minoritised communities receive disproportionately less funding than their white-led counterparts even when they are delivering equally vital or more impactful work in their communities. The Step Forward Fund directly challenges this imbalance by: Prioritizing organizations that have historically been underserved or excluded from larger funding streams Centering lived experience of structural racism and oppression as a core eligibility criterion Using language co-developed with Global Majority and racially minoritised communities themselves Partnering with The Phoenix Community Trust and their networks to ensure community power is embedded in the fund s design and governance What Is Meant by Global Majority and Racially Minoritised Communities ? The Step Forward Fund uses specific language that has been carefully chosen in consultation with the communities the fund is designed to serve. The term Global Majority and racially minoritised communities includes, but is not limited to, the following groups: Black, African, and Caribbean communities People of any mixed or multiple ethnic background Asian communities Arab communities Jewish communities Gypsy, Roma, and Traveller communities Importantly, this funding is specifically intended for people and organizations with lived experiences of structural racism and oppression. Lived experience in this context refers to knowledge gained through direct, first-hand, and personal experience not simply professional knowledge or academic understanding of these issues. The language used in this fund has been developed through research with these communities and agreed upon with The Phoenix Community Trust and their partners. The National Lottery Community Fund uses this language because the communities themselves are the authority on how they wish to be described and represented. How Much Funding Is Available? The Step Forward Fund has a total of 4 million pounds available to award across England. The funding is structured as follows: Grants are provided at a rate of 50,000 to 75,000 pounds per year Funding periods run for between 2 and 3 years The minimum grant available is 100,000 pounds (50,000 pounds per year for 2 years) The maximum grant available is 225,000 pounds (75,000 pounds per year for 3 years) The National Lottery Community Fund expects to make between 20 and 30 awards in total This multi-year funding model is significant. Rather than offering one-off project grants, The Step Forward Fund provides sustained investment that gives organizations the financial stability and time needed to genuinely develop their capacity, implement longer-term plans, and grow in a sustainable way. Who Can Apply: Eligibility Criteria To be eligible for The Step Forward Fund, an organization must meet all of the following criteria: Have received a small grant of 20,000 pounds or less from The National Lottery Community Fund between April 2023 and March 2026 Have applied to the Reaching Communities fund between April 2023 and March 2026 and been unsuccessful in that application Have a board or leadership team where the majority of members are from Global Majority and racially minoritised communities Prioritize services and activities specifically for Global Majority and racially minoritised communities Have an annual income of less than 300,000 pounds per annum, based on a three-year average These criteria have been set deliberately to target organizations that are at a specific and recognizable stage of their development organizations that have already demonstrated their value and potential by receiving some National Lottery funding, that have shown ambition by applying for larger grants through Reaching Communities, and that are now ready to take a meaningful step forward with the right level of support. Who Cannot Apply Applications from the following will not be considered: Individual people this fund is exclusively for organizations, not individuals Organizations that do not have a majority of board or leadership members from Global Majority and racially minoritised communities Organizations whose primary focus is not on services and activities for Global Majority and racially minoritised communities Organizations based outside of England this fund is for England only and does not apply in Northern Ireland, Scotland, or Wales Organizations with an annual income of 300,000 pounds or more Organizations that did not receive a small National Lottery Community Fund grant between April 2023 and March 2026 Organizations that did not apply to Reaching Communities between April 2023 and March 2026 The Funding Aims of The Step Forward Fund The Step Forward Fund has been designed with clear and purposeful aims that go beyond simply providing money. The broader purpose and specific aims of the fund are as follows: The overall purpose is to: Provide funding and practical support to grassroots organizations from Global Majority and racially minoritised communities Help them transition from smaller, project-based funding cycles to larger grants and sustained investment The specific funding aims are to: Invest in the genuine growth and organizational development of grassroots organizations Provide encouragement and support to organizations that want to deliver on longer-term strategic plans Generate learning that improves The National Lottery Community Fund s own grant-making practice Influence positive and systemic changes across the wider funding and community development sector What the Funding Can Be Used For The Step Forward Fund is intended to support organizational growth and sustainability, not just project delivery. While specific eligible expenditure will be detailed in the application guidelines, the fund is broadly designed to help organizations: Develop and strengthen their organizational infrastructure and capacity Invest in staff, leadership, and governance Sustain and expand the services and activities they provide to their communities Build the systems, processes, and partnerships needed for long-term impact Advance their ability to engage with larger funding streams in the future Applicants are encouraged to review the full guidance on the National Lottery Community Fund website for detailed information on eligible spending categories. What the Fund Is Looking for in Applications The Step Forward Fund wants to see applications that clearly demonstrate: The organization s roots in and connection to Global Majority and racially minoritised communities The lived experience of structural racism and oppression among the organization s leadership and community A clear vision for how the funding will enable the organization to grow and deepen its impact Evidence of existing work and positive outcomes for the community A credible and realistic plan for how the multi-year grant will be used Alignment with the fund s aims around organizational development, sustained advocacy, and community care How to Apply Ap plications for The Step Forward Fund must be submitted through The National Lottery Community Fund s application portal. The programme is currently open to applications. Key application details: Project location: England only Grant amount: Between 100,000 and 225,000 pounds Decision timeline: Applicants can expect a decision within approximately 21 weeks of applying Programme status: Open to applications Prospective applicants are strongly encouraged to read all available guidance on the National Lottery Community Fund website before beginning their application, and to make use of any available support resources to ensure their application is as strong as possible. For organizations that have previously been unsuccessful in applying for larger funding, or that feel uncertain about whether they qualify, the fund s co-design with The Phoenix Community Trust and their partners means there is genuine community expertise embedded in the programme to support the process. APPLY HERE After You Apply Once an application has been submitted, The National Lottery Community Fund will review it for eligibility and quality. Organizations can expect to receive a decision within approximately 21 weeks. Successful organizations will receive multi-year funding and the support needed to begin implementing their plans. The fund is also designed to generate learning meaning that The National Lottery Community Fund will be actively working with funded organizations to understand what is working, what challenges arise, and how the experience of this fund can improve future grant-making practice and influence the wider sector. Visit HERE for more information about the Step Forward Fund 2026. Conclusion The Step Forward Fund represents a genuine and substantive commitment to equity in community funding in England. For grassroots organizations led by and serving Global Majority and racially minoritised communities that have been navigating the challenging gap between small project grants and the larger investment they need to grow, this fund could be genuinely transformational. With between 100,000 and 225,000 pounds available per organization, a multi-year funding model that allows for real organizational development, and a design process that has centered the voices and language of the communities it serves, The Step Forward Fund is a rare opportunity to take a meaningful step forward. If your organization has received a small National Lottery grant, has tried and not yet succeeded with a Reaching Communities application, is led by and serves Global Majority and racially minoritised communities, and has an annual income below 300,000 pounds, then this fund was designed with you in mind. The programme is currently open to applications. Review the full eligibility criteria on The National Lottery Community Fund website, prepare a thoughtful and detailed application, and take the step forward your organization has been working toward. Visit OFY to discover more global Grant opportunities. The post Step Forward Fund 2026: National Lottery Grants for Grassroots Organizations in England appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Goldman Sachs Community Development Champions Grant 2026: Everything Nonprofit Organizations in New York, New Jersey, and Utah Need to Know
summary: Goldman Sachs, one of the world s most recognized financial institutions, is once again opening its Community Development Grants program for 2026 and nonprofit organizations working to advance economic opportunity in low- and moderate-income communities across New York, New Jersey, and Utah should take note. Through two distinct funding initiatives the Change Makers Initiative and the Equity Builders Initiative Goldman Sachs is providing both one-time and multi-year general operating grants to high-impact nonprofits committed to transforming the lives of individuals and families in underserved communities. This article provides a thorough and detailed breakdown of the Goldman Sachs Community Development Champions Grant program for 2026, covering what it is, the two initiatives on offer, who is eligible, what the assessment criteria are, how the funding works, the timeline, and how to apply. What Is the Goldman Sachs Community Development Champions Program? The Goldman Sachs Community Development Champions program is a philanthropic grant initiative rooted in the firm s long-standing belief that strong communities are the foundation of economic growth and a prosperous society. Through its Community Development Grants program, Goldman Sachs provides funding to nonprofit organizations that are committed to advancing economic opportunity and mobility in low- and moderate-income (LMI) communities across the United States. The programme has already demonstrated meaningful impact. According to self-reported data from 2025 grantees collected in January 2026, the program has: Reached over 808,000 community members Served more than 2,000 small businesses Supported 30 grant champions across its initiatives These figures reflect the scale and depth of Goldman Sachs s investment in community development and the 2026 cycle represents the next chapter in that commitment. The Four Pillars of Goldman Sachs Community Development The Goldman Sachs Community Development Grants program is structured around four core areas of focus, all of which are designed to address the most pressing and interconnected challenges facing low- and moderate-income communities: 1. Affordable Housing Solutions This pillar focuses on providing access to affordable, safe, and stable housing for LMI individuals and families. Housing instability is one of the most significant barriers to economic mobility, and Goldman Sachs recognizes that sustainable community development must begin with ensuring that people have a secure place to live. 2. Essential Community Services This pillar encompasses a broad range of critical support systems, including social services, education, job training, and broader community development initiatives. The goal is to ensure that LMI individuals have access to the services and skills they need to participate fully in the economy and society. 3. Small Business Growth This pillar focuses on providing catalytic capital and philanthropic investment in economic development, with a particular emphasis on under-resourced small businesses. Small businesses are the backbone of local economies, and targeted support for entrepreneurs in LMI communities can generate jobs, build wealth, and revitalize neighborhoods. 4. Neighborhood Revitalization This pillar supports activities that help attract new businesses and residents to underserved neighborhoods or retain existing ones. Neighborhood revitalization is about creating conditions for long-term, sustainable community prosperity not just addressing immediate needs. The Two Grant Initiatives: Change Makers and Equity Builders The Goldman Sachs Community Development Champions program for 2026 operates through two distinct funding initiatives, each with a different structure and purpose. Both are currently open for applications. Initiative 1: The Change Makers Initiative The Change Makers Initiative is an annual funding opportunity that provides a one-time general operating grant to high-impact nonprofit organizations located in New York, New Jersey, and Utah. This initiative is designed for nonprofits that are driving innovation and measurable impact in LMI communities through differentiated approaches and community-driven solutions. Key themes that have defined recent Change Makers cohorts include: Access to Housing: Expediting access to affordable apartments for homeless New Yorkers with housing vouchers Embracing AI: Leading artificial intelligence adoption initiatives and ensuring LMI communities are not left behind in the digital economy, through literacy training and organizational transformation Empowering Parents: Providing free, high-quality childcare that enables parents to work toward financial autonomy and security Building Financial Futures: Offering financial coaching and debt relief services that open pathways to homeownership and long-term economic stability Past Change Makers grantees include a wide range of recognized organizations such as Brooklyn Public Library (LevelUp), DataKind, iMentor NYC, Rising Tide Capital, Neighborhood Trust Financial Partners, The HOPE Program, BRICK Networks, DreamYard Project, Van Alen Institute, and many others across New York, New Jersey, and Utah. Initiative 2: The Equity Builders Initiative The Equity Builders Initiative provides multi-year, general operating grants to nonprofit organizations working to build organizational capacity and sustainably deepen their impact in the LMI communities they serve. Unlike the Change Makers one-time grant, Equity Builders is designed for organizations that are ready to take a longer-term view of their growth and impact and that need sustained, flexible investment to do so. Multi-year general operating grants are among the most valuable forms of philanthropic support available to nonprofits, because they provide the financial stability and flexibility needed to invest in staff, systems, leadership, and long-term strategic planning not just individual programmes or projects. Both initiatives are currently open for applications. Eligible organizations should carefully review both options and determine which initiative best aligns with their organizational stage and funding needs. Who Is Eligible to Apply? To be considered for either the Change Makers or Equity Builders initiative, applicants must meet all of the following eligibility criteria: Be a domestic, nonprofit, tax-exempt organization registered under Section 501(c)(3) of the United States Internal Revenue Code Have a clear and demonstrable focus on community development, specifically addressing the needs of LMI areas or households Operate primarily in one of the following specified geographic regions: New York: New York County, Bronx County, Kings County, Richmond County, or Queens County New Jersey: Hudson County or Essex County Utah: Salt Lake County, Summit County, or Tooele County These geographic parameters are important. The grant is not available to nonprofits operating outside of these specific counties, even if they are based in the broader states of New York, New Jersey, or Utah. Organizations must confirm that their primary area of operation falls within the designated counties before applying. Assessment Criteria: What Goldman Sachs Is Looking For Applications to the Goldman Sachs Community Development Champions Grant will be evaluated based on but not limited to the following three core criteria: 1. Impact Applications should specify measurable outcomes in LMI communities Applicants must demonstrate a documented history of effectively meeting community needs Goldman Sachs is looking for evidence that the organization s work produces real, quantifiable change in the lives of LMI individuals and families 2. Community Engagement Applications should demonstrate meaningful engagement with LMI communities throughout the design and implementation of programming Goldman Sachs prioritizes community-driven solutions meaning that the people most affected by the challenges being addressed should be active participants in shaping the solutions Organizations that position community members as partners rather than passive recipients of services will be viewed favorably 3. Innovation Applications should demonstrate a differentiated approach to community development Applicants should articulate clearly how their model advances beyond existing solutions and delivers measurable impact for LMI communities Goldman Sachs is particularly interested in organizations that are finding new and more effective ways to address longstanding challenges in housing, employment, financial inclusion, education, and small business development Application Timeline The Goldman Sachs Community Development Champions Grant operates on the following timeline for the 2026 cycle: Phase 1 Application Submission: All eligible applicants must submit their completed application by July 24, 2026, at 11:59 PM Eastern Standard Time (EST). This is a firm deadline and late submissions will not be considered. Phase 2 Award Decisions: Award decisions will be finalized in the fourth quarter of 2026. Successful awardees will be notified ahead of a public announcement. Phase 3 Funding Disbursement: Funding will be disbursed to awarded nonprofits by the end of 2026, for implementation and programming throughout 2027. How to Apply Organizations wishing to apply to either the Change Makers or Equity Builders initiative should: Review the 2026 Application Questions PDF, available on the Goldman Sachs Community Development Champions webpage Consult the Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) document for additional guidance and clarification Submit applications through the official online application portal by the deadline of July 24, 2026, at 11:59 PM EST For any additional questions not addressed in the FAQ, prospective applicants can reach out directly to the Goldman Sachs Community Development team at: gs-cd-grants@gs.com You can find the 2026 Application Questions PDF here . Read More and Apply Here Why This Grant Matters for LMI Communities General operating grants of the kind offered through both the Change Makers and Equity Builders initiatives are widely regarded as the most impactful and flexible form of philanthropic support that a nonprofit organization can receive. Unlike restricted project grants that can only be spent on specific activities, general operating grants give organizations the freedom to use the funding where it is most needed whether that is hiring staff, upgrading technology, improving financial management, expanding programmes, or building reserves for resilience. For nonprofits working in LMI communities in New York City, New Jersey, and Utah, where the cost of living is high and the funding landscape is competitive, a Goldman Sachs Community Development grant can be genuinely transformational. Beyond the financial support, selection as a Goldman Sachs Community Development Champion brings significant reputational recognition and the opportunity to be part of a network of high-impact organizations working toward a common goal of economic opportunity and mobility for all. Conclusion The Goldman Sachs Community Development Champions Grant 2026 is a compelling and high-value funding opportunity for nonprofit organizations in New York, New Jersey, and Utah that are doing serious, measurable work to advance economic opportunity in low- and moderate-income communities. Whether through the one-time general operating grant of the Change Makers Initiative or the sustained, multi-year investment of the Equity Builders Initiative, Goldman Sachs is offering nonprofits the kind of flexible, unrestricted funding that can truly move the needle on community development. With a track record of reaching over 808,000 community members and serving more than 2,000 small businesses through past grantees, the program has a proven record of supporting organizations that are making a real and lasting difference. The application deadline of July 24, 2026 at 11:59 PM EST is firm, and competition is expected to be strong. Eligible nonprofits should begin preparing their applications immediately, ensuring they can clearly demonstrate measurable impact, deep community engagement, and an innovative approach that goes beyond existing solutions. This is your opportunity to join a cohort of community development champions backed by one of the world s most prominent financial institutions do not miss it. Discover more global Grant opportunities on OFY. The post Goldman Sachs Community Development Champions Grant 2026: Everything Nonprofit Organizations in New York, New Jersey, and Utah Need to Know appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: NEXUS 2026: German Marshall Fund Launches New Security Leadership Programme for MA and PhD Students in the US, Japan, and Europe
summary: The German Marshall Fund of the United States has launched a new leadership initiative called NEXUS (Next Generation Security Exchange) , designed to prepare the next generation of security experts and policymakers working on nuclear issues and international cooperation. Funded by The Japan Foundation, NEXUS brings together emerging scholars from the United States, Japan, and Europe for an intensive learning experience focused on nuclear security, strategic affairs, and international cooperation. The programme is now accepting applications from outstanding Master’s and PhD students who want to deepen their understanding of nuclear policy while building professional networks across regions. Application Deadline: 30 June 2026 at 11:59 p.m. ET About the NEXUS Programme NEXUS: Next Generation Security Exchange is a new leadership programme created to develop future leaders who can contribute to stronger: US-Japan bilateral relations. US-Japan-Europe trilateral cooperation. Global nuclear security discussions. International policy development. The initiative focuses on preparing participants to become informed researchers, analysts, and policymakers who understand the complex security challenges shaping the world today. The programme’s goal is to build a network of emerging experts who can contribute to future conversations on nuclear strategy, international security, and cooperation among key global partners. Programme Objectives NEXUS aims to: Train future security professionals. Strengthen cooperation between the United States, Japan, and Europe. Develop deeper understanding of nuclear security challenges. Encourage cross-regional collaboration among young researchers. Support participants in becoming future policy leaders. The programme recognizes the importance of developing a new generation of experts capable of addressing complex global security issues through research, dialogue, and cooperation. Who Will Be Selected? For its inaugural year, NEXUS will select: 10 Master’s and PhD students from: Japan. Europe. The United States. Selected participants will form a diverse international cohort focused on exploring nuclear security issues and strengthening cooperation between regions. Key Topics Covered During the Programme Participants will explore major areas of nuclear policy and international security, including: Nuclear Deterrence Strategy Students will examine the role of nuclear deterrence in international relations and how strategic decisions influence global security. Topics may include: Security guarantees. Military strategy. Regional security dynamics. Strategic stability. International Nuclear History Participants will study historical developments that have shaped today’s nuclear landscape. This includes understanding: Major nuclear events. Evolution of nuclear policies. Lessons from past international security challenges. Nuclear Nonproliferation The programme will explore efforts to prevent the spread of nuclear weapons and strengthen international security frameworks. Participants will examine: Nonproliferation agreements. Global security mechanisms. Challenges facing nuclear governance. Alliance Politics Students will analyze how partnerships between countries influence security decision-making. Special focus will be placed on: US-Japan relations. US-Japan-Europe cooperation. Regional security partnerships. Programme Format The NEXUS programme combines online learning with an in-person immersive experience. The programme includes: Interactive Webinars Participants will take part in a series of virtual sessions designed to build knowledge, encourage discussion, and connect students with experts. In-Person Training in Washington, DC The virtual sessions will lead to an intensive in-person training programme in: Washington, DC Date: 8–13 November 2026 The in-person programme will provide opportunities for: Direct engagement with experts. Professional networking. Policy discussions. Practical learning experiences. Participant Benefits Selected participants will receive support to participate in the programme. Benefits include: Round-trip airfare to Washington, DC. Accommodation. Meals during the programme. Access to expert-led training. Opportunity to build international professional networks. Exposure to security policy discussions. Opportunity to publish a short policy-relevant article. The programme provides participants with both academic knowledge and practical experience relevant to future careers in security and policymaking. Publication Opportunity Participants will have the opportunity to develop and publish a short policy-focused article. This allows participants to: Share their ideas with broader audiences. Develop policy communication skills. Strengthen their professional profiles. Application Requirements Applicants must submit: 1. Resume Requirements: Maximum length: 2 pages. The resume should highlight: Academic background. Research experience. Relevant professional activities. Security or policy interests. 2. Statement of Interest Applicants must provide a short statement explaining: Why they want to participate in NEXUS. How they plan to apply knowledge gained from the programme. How nuclear policy and trilateral cooperation connect with their future professional goals. The statement should also confirm: Ability to travel to Washington, DC from 8–13 November 2026. Commitment to attending all virtual sessions. For applicants based in Europe and Japan: They should indicate whether they have previously visited Washington, DC. Statement length: Maximum 250 words Application Timeline Important dates: Application deadline: 30 June 2026 at 11:59 p.m. ET Virtual sessions: Before the in-person training Washington, DC training: 8–13 November 2026 Applicants should prepare their materials carefully and submit before the deadline. How to Apply Interested students should apply through the German Marshall Fund career application platform. Click here for more and to apply Applicants should submit their resume and statement of interest through the application process. References are not required. Applicants may enter “N/A” in reference sections where applicable. Programme Experts The programme is supported by experts from the German Marshall Fund’s Indo-Pacific team, including: Sayuri Romei Colleen Scribner Jennifer Nguyen Why This Opportunity Matters NEXUS represents an important opportunity for emerging researchers who want to contribute to conversations about nuclear security and international cooperation. By bringing together students from the United States, Japan, and Europe, the programme creates a platform for developing future leaders who can address global challenges through collaboration, research, and informed policy-making. Conclusion The NEXUS: Next Generation Security Exchange programme offers MA and PhD students a unique opportunity to expand their expertise in nuclear security, connect with international peers, and engage with leading experts in global affairs. Students interested in security studies, international relations, and nuclear policy are encouraged to apply before the 30 June 2026 deadline . Click here for more global fellowship opportunities The post NEXUS 2026: German Marshall Fund Launches New Security Leadership Programme for MA and PhD Students in the US, Japan, and Europe appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Dinah F.B. Cohen Fellowship Program Fall 2026: Paid Leadership and Professional Skills Opportunity for Students and Recent Graduates with Disabilities in the United States
summary: The Dinah F.B. Cohen Fellowship Program is a paid professional development and leadership initiative designed to empower college students and recent graduates with disabilities. The program focuses on building practical skills, leadership capacity, and career readiness while honoring the legacy of Dinah F.B. Cohen. This fellowship is particularly committed to supporting young adults with disabilities, especially those who belong to multiply-marginalized groups—individuals who experience overlapping forms of discrimination and social exclusion due to multiple minority identities. The program provides meaningful hands-on experience, mentorship, and financial compensation through a structured short-term fellowship engagement. Overview of the Dinah F.B. Cohen Fellowship Program The fellowship aims to bridge the gap between academic learning and professional experience for individuals with disabilities by offering structured exposure to real-world work environments. Key objectives include: Strengthening professional and leadership skills among participants Providing equitable access to career development opportunities Supporting inclusion of people with disabilities in leadership spaces Promoting representation of multiply-marginalized communities in professional environments Participants are selected through a competitive national review process and engage in a short-term, paid fellowship experience. Fellowship Structure and Duration Selected fellows will participate in a structured professional program that includes: Approximately 65 total hours of fellowship work Duration: September to December 2026 Weekly commitment: 5–6 hours per week Program length: Approximately 12 weeks This flexible structure allows participants to balance academic, professional, or personal commitments while gaining meaningful experience. Financial Support and Stipend The fellowship is a paid opportunity. Upon successful completion of all fellowship requirements, participants will receive: A total stipend of $1,500 This compensation is provided as recognition of the fellow’s time, contributions, and successful completion of the program. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: General Eligibility Must be a college student or a recent graduate Must have a documented disability Must be eligible for employment in the United States without requiring visa sponsorship or visa approval Additional Considerations Strong encouragement for individuals from multiply-marginalized backgrounds Applicants from groups experiencing overlapping systems of discrimination are highly encouraged to apply Selection Process and Timeline The fellowship follows a structured national review process: Application Deadline: July 31st, 2026 at 9:00 PM EST Review Period: August to September 2026 Notification Period: Late August to early September 2026 Fellowship Period: September to December 2026 Applicants are evaluated through a competitive selection process, and only selected candidates will be invited to participate. Benefits of the Fellowship This program offers several professional and personal development advantages, including: Paid professional experience in a structured environment Leadership development opportunities Exposure to real-world organizational work Skill-building in communication, teamwork, and project execution Networking opportunities with professionals and peers A $1,500 completion stipend Enhanced career readiness for future employment opportunities Application Information Interested candidates can apply through the official fellowship page: Dinah F.B. Cohen Fellowship Program Application Applicants are encouraged to prepare strong application materials highlighting their academic background, lived experiences, leadership potential, and motivation for joining the fellowship. Why This Fellowship Matters This fellowship is more than a short-term professional opportunity. It represents a commitment to inclusive leadership development by ensuring that individuals with disabilities—especially those from underrepresented and multiply-marginalized backgrounds—have access to structured, paid career-building experiences. By participating, fellows not only gain skills but also contribute to a broader movement toward equity, representation, and inclusion in professional spaces. Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post Dinah F.B. Cohen Fellowship Program Fall 2026: Paid Leadership and Professional Skills Opportunity for Students and Recent Graduates with Disabilities in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UK Youth Trustee Recruitment 2026: Join the Board and Help Shape the Future of Young People Across the UK
summary: Are you passionate about creating better opportunities for young people? UK Youth is inviting applications for four new Trustee positions in 2026, offering individuals the opportunity to influence strategy, strengthen youth services, and help shape the future of youth work across the United Kingdom. The opportunity is designed for people who want to contribute their experience, expertise, and lived knowledge to an organisation committed to ensuring every young person has access to support, development opportunities, and positive youth experiences. UK Youth is seeking Trustees who can strengthen and diversify its Board, champion youth voice, support effective governance, and help deliver the organisation’s ambitious new strategy. Application Deadline: 17 July 2026 About UK Youth UK Youth works to create a society where every young person is supported through every stage of their journey — from moments of opportunity and growth to challenges and uncertainty. The organisation believes that youth work and outdoor learning have the power to transform lives by helping young people develop: Confidence. Skills. Resilience. Leadership abilities. Positive relationships. Strong connections with their communities. As the UK’s national infrastructure body for youth work, UK Youth supports and strengthens a diverse network of youth organisations, helps unlock investment, influences policy, and builds evidence around effective youth programmes. Overview of the Trustee Opportunity UK Youth is recruiting four new Trustees in 2026 to strengthen Board expertise, improve representation, embed youth voice, and support the organisation’s future direction. Successful Trustees will play an important role in: Providing strategic leadership. Supporting good governance. Helping shape organisational priorities. Ensuring young people remain central to decision-making. Bringing specialist knowledge and lived experience to Board discussions. This is an opportunity for individuals who want to contribute to meaningful social change and help improve opportunities for young people throughout the UK. Available Trustee Positions UK Youth is looking for candidates for four specific Trustee roles. 1. Two Young Trustees (Aged 18–22) UK Youth is seeking two young Trustees who can bring youth perspectives and lived experience into Board decision-making. Ideal candidates should have: Experience of youth work. Engagement with youth programmes. Experience in the youth sector or outdoor learning sector. Understanding of challenges and opportunities affecting young people. Applicants are also encouraged to demonstrate skills or experience gained through: Education. Volunteering. Employment. Community involvement. Personal lived experience. These roles aim to ensure young people have a meaningful voice in shaping the organisation’s future. 2. Trustee With Grant-Making Expertise One Trustee position is available for someone with professional experience in grant-making and funding. Applicants should have knowledge or experience in areas such as: Developing funding strategies. Grant-making governance. Ensuring fairness and equity in funding decisions. Measuring impact. Maintaining accountability. This Trustee will help strengthen UK Youth’s approach to investment and funding decisions. 3. Trustee With Outdoor Learning Operations and Safeguarding Expertise UK Youth is also seeking a Trustee with specialist knowledge of outdoor learning programmes and safeguarding. The ideal candidate should understand: How to deliver safe outdoor learning experiences. Management of residential programmes. Operational leadership. Safeguarding responsibilities. Creating high-quality experiences for young people. This expertise will support UK Youth’s ambitions around developing outdoor learning opportunities. Why Join UK Youth as a Trustee? Becoming a Trustee provides an opportunity to make a lasting difference by contributing to an organisation focused on improving young people’s lives. Benefits of joining include: Influence Positive Change Trustees help guide decisions that affect youth organisations and young people across the UK. Use Your Skills for Social Impact Your professional knowledge, personal experience, or community insight can help strengthen youth services. Support Youth Voice Trustees help ensure young people’s experiences and perspectives influence important decisions. Contribute to Future Strategy UK Youth is entering an important phase focused on: Long-term organisational resilience. Income diversification. Expanding impact. Developing outdoor learning opportunities. Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion UK Youth is committed to building a Board that reflects the communities it serves. The organisation particularly encourages applications from people who: Come from global majority backgrounds. Live outside London. Have experience working across the four nations of the UK. Identify as disabled. UK Youth believes diverse leadership strengthens decision-making and helps ensure that different experiences and perspectives are represented. Trustee Responsibilities All Trustees will be expected to: Support UK Youth’s mission and values. Provide strategic oversight. Participate in Board discussions. Uphold strong governance standards. Follow the Nolan Principles for Standards in Public Life. Help guide the organisation toward achieving its goals. UK Youth’s Future Strategy The organisation is entering a new stage of development focused on creating stronger foundations for youth work. Key strategic priorities include: Building long-term resilience. Diversifying income streams. Expanding investment opportunities. Delivering a transformational Capital Appeal. Developing Avon Tyrrell as a national centre of excellence for outdoor learning. Trustees will play a key role in supporting this future vision. How to Apply Interested candidates should: Review the Trustee Recruitment Pack. Understand the available Trustee roles. Complete the required application materials. Submit the diversity monitoring form. Apply before the closing date. Click here for more and to apply Application Deadline The deadline for applications is: 17 July 2026 Applicants are encouraged to apply early and carefully demonstrate how their skills, experience, and values align with UK Youth’s mission. Conclusion The UK Youth Trustee opportunity is a chance to contribute to meaningful change by helping shape the future of youth work across the United Kingdom. Whether through lived experience, professional expertise, governance knowledge, or passion for youth development, successful Trustees will help ensure more young people can access opportunities that support their growth, confidence, and success. Click here for more global job opportunities The post UK Youth Trustee Recruitment 2026: Join the Board and Help Shape the Future of Young People Across the UK appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Build a Career That Matters at KPMG Ghana: Explore Graduate, Internship, and Experienced Career Opportunities
summary: Finding the right employer is one of the most important decisions in any professional journey. For individuals seeking meaningful work, continuous learning, and opportunities to create lasting impact, KPMG Ghana offers a dynamic workplace where innovation, collaboration, and professional excellence come together. As one of the world s leading professional services firms, KPMG is committed to empowering talented individuals, driving innovation, and helping businesses, governments, and communities solve complex challenges. Whether you are an experienced professional looking for your next leadership opportunity, a recent graduate preparing to launch your career, or a student seeking practical industry experience, KPMG Ghana provides multiple career pathways designed to help you grow and thrive. Through its people-centered culture, comprehensive learning opportunities, and commitment to inclusion and diversity, KPMG Ghana continues to attract ambitious professionals who are passionate about making a real difference. About KPMG Ghana KPMG Ghana is part of the global KPMG organization, an international network of independent member firms providing professional services in: Audit Tax Advisory Consulting Risk Management Business Transformation Digital Solutions With a global heritage spanning more than 150 years, KPMG supports organizations across industries by helping them transform, scale their operations, build trust, manage risk, and adapt to an increasingly digital world. At the heart of KPMG s success is its people. The organization believes that empowering talented professionals with the right skills, technology, and collaborative environment creates long-term value for clients and society. Why Build Your Career at KPMG Ghana? KPMG Ghana places significant emphasis on creating an environment where employees can excel professionally while maintaining their personal wellbeing. Its Employee Value Proposition focuses on four key pillars. Do Work That Matters Employees have the opportunity to work on projects that create meaningful impact for businesses, governments, and communities. Professionals contribute to solving complex business challenges while supporting sustainable growth and innovation across multiple sectors. Working at KPMG means becoming part of an organization dedicated to creating lasting value through professional excellence. Come As You Are KPMG Ghana promotes an inclusive workplace where diversity is valued and every employee is encouraged to contribute their unique perspectives. The organization champions Inclusion, Diversity, and Equity (IDE), creating a culture built on: Trust Respect Equal opportunity Collaboration Inclusive leadership Employees are encouraged to bring their authentic selves to work every day. Thrive with Us Employee wellbeing is recognized as a critical component of long-term success. KPMG Ghana supports flexible working arrangements and promotes wellbeing across multiple dimensions, including: Mental health Physical wellbeing Financial wellbeing Workplace flexibility Healthy work-life integration Learn for a Lifetime Continuous learning is a core part of the KPMG experience. Employees benefit from: Global digital learning platforms Professional certifications Function-specific technical training Leadership development programmes Learning from experienced professionals International secondment opportunities Exchange programmes These opportunities help employees continually develop new skills and remain competitive in an evolving professional landscape. Employee Benefits KPMG Ghana offers a competitive benefits package designed to support employees throughout their careers. Benefits include: Competitive salary Paid leave Comprehensive health benefits Flexible working arrangements Employee engagement activities Employee recognition and awards Career development opportunities Professional learning programmes International exposure through secondments and exchanges These benefits demonstrate the organization s commitment to attracting and retaining top talent. Career Opportunities at KPMG Ghana KPMG Ghana provides several career pathways to accommodate professionals at different stages of their careers. Experienced Hires Professionals with prior industry or consulting experience can join KPMG Ghana to expand their expertise, work with diverse clients, and contribute to high-impact projects across multiple industries. Experienced hires benefit from opportunities for leadership development, career progression, and exposure to complex business challenges. Graduate Trainee Programme Recent graduates can launch their professional careers through KPMG Ghana s Graduate Trainee Programme. The programme is designed to help graduates transition from academic learning to professional practice by providing practical experience, structured development, mentorship, and exposure to real client engagements. Graduates work alongside experienced professionals while building technical expertise and professional competencies that support long-term career success. KPMG Internship Program Students seeking practical work experience can apply for internship opportunities at KPMG Ghana. Internships provide valuable exposure to professional services, allowing participants to develop workplace skills, understand client engagements, and gain insights into the consulting and business advisory industry. The internship programme also enables participants to build professional networks and strengthen their future employability. Career Development at KPMG Ghana Career development is an ongoing priority at KPMG Ghana. The organization encourages: Regular career conversations Continuous feedback Internal mobility Cross-functional learning Leadership development Skills expansion Long-term career planning Employees are supported in exploring new opportunities within the organization while expanding their expertise across different business areas. Life at KPMG Ghana KPMG believes that strong teams produce stronger results. Employees collaborate in an environment that encourages innovation, mutual respect, and knowledge sharing. The firm s culture supports teamwork, professional growth, and continuous improvement, making it an attractive destination for ambitious professionals who want to build rewarding careers. Why Consider KPMG Ghana? Choosing KPMG Ghana means joining an organization that invests in its people through learning, mentorship, innovation, and inclusive leadership. Whether you are beginning your professional journey or seeking your next career move, KPMG Ghana provides opportunities to develop technical expertise, expand your professional network, and contribute to meaningful projects that create lasting impact. Professionals interested in building successful careers in consulting, advisory, audit, tax, and related fields are encouraged to regularly explore available opportunities on KPMG Ghana s official careers portal, as application timelines vary by programme and position. Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Job opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post Build a Career That Matters at KPMG Ghana: Explore Graduate, Internship, and Experienced Career Opportunities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Launch Your Federal Career: U.S. Department of State Pathways Student Trainee Program in Program Analysis
summary: Launch Your Federal Career: U.S. Department of State Pathways Student Trainee Program in Program Analysis The U.S. Department of State has officially opened applications for its Pathways Student Trainee (Program Analysis) positions within the Bureau of Consular Affairs, Overseas Citizens Services (OCS). This highly competitive program offers students paid opportunities to explore federal careers while completing their education. Positions are located in Washington, D.C., with 7 vacancies currently available. This program serves as a critical talent pipeline. Upon successful completion of all academic and program requirements, participants may be eligible for a non-competitive conversion into a permanent federal career or a career-conditional position within the competitive civil service. Critical Application and Deadline Information Applicants must note that this vacancy announcement operates under strict application limits. The application window is open from June 25, 2026, to July 1, 2026. However, the announcement will close at 11:59 p.m. Eastern Time on July 1, 2026, OR on the exact day that the 100th application package is received—whichever condition is met first. If this 100-application threshold is reached on the opening day, the announcement will close immediately that same day. Interested candidates are strongly urged to submit their complete application packages as early as possible. Compensation, Location, and Work Schedule Salary Range: $57,736 to $91,815 per year. Pay Scale and Grade: GS-07 to GS-09. Location: Washington, D.C. Appointment Type: Internships (A non-temporary excepted service appointment expected to last more than one year). Work Schedule: Full-time is highly preferred. However, a minimum of 40 hours per biweekly pay period is required to maintain eligibility in the program. Core hours typically require arrival in the morning and departure 8.45 hours later (standard hours are generally 8:15 a.m. to 5:00 p.m.). Telework: Situational telework agreements may be permitted, subject to supervisory approval. Travel: Not required, with only occasional travel if specific needs arise. Role Overviews and Bureau Divisions Selected trainees will be embedded within the Bureau of Consular Affairs, Directorate of Overseas Citizens Services. OCS is responsible for creating policy and monitoring both emergency and non-emergency protective services for American citizens living or traveling abroad. Vacancies are distributed across three primary office environments: 1. The OCS Front Office (CA/OCS/FO) Trainees in this unit assist with managing the production of written information, action items, and informational memoranda flowing from approximately 200 employees across OCS. This material travels through the Consular Affairs Front Office up to Seventh Floor Principals. Trainees act as direct informational liaisons, review draft materials, and alert leadership to significant diplomatic events or high-profile issues. 2. The Office of American Citizens Services and Crisis Management (CA/OCS/ACS) Trainees are assigned a broad spectrum of operational duties to help oversee day-to-day consular operations for all U.S. embassies and consulates globally. This office designs and implements the precise diplomatic initiatives that protect U.S. nationals abroad during major international crises or routine emergency scenarios. 3. The Management Support Unit (CA/OCS/MSU) Trainees in this unit report directly to the Unit Chief, contributing to strategic planning, Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) processing, workforce management, data sharing, data integration, business analytics, and website support. This office also coordinates federal benefits/obligations programs abroad and manages the OCS After-Hours Crisis Duty Program. Strict Position Requirements and Conditions of Employment To maintain eligibility and consideration for these federal roles, all candidates must meet the following baseline conditions: Citizenship: S. citizenship is strictly required. Age: Applicants must be at least 16 years of age by the closing date of the announcement. Background Check and Security: Candidates must be able to obtain and maintain a Secret security clearance. The vetting process involves a background check, credentialing, and suitability/fitness reviews. Drug Testing: The incumbent will be subject to random drug testing throughout their tenure. Employment Verification: Candidates must successfully pass the E-Verify employment verification check. Academic Standing: A cumulative Grade Point Average (GPA) of 2.0 or higher on a 4.0 scale must be maintained for the entire duration of the Pathways appointment. Enrollment Status: Applicants must be currently enrolled or accepted for enrollment at an accredited educational institution with at least a half-time course load. Student eligibility must remain active throughout the internship. Graduation Timeline: All submitted transcripts or official academic documentation must explicitly reflect a projected graduation date after the Spring 2027 semester or later. Educational Qualifications by Grade Level Applicants will be evaluated and placed into specific GS pay grades based on their academic achievements by the closing date of the announcement: GS-07 Level Candidates must meet at least one of the following criteria: Completion of one full academic year of graduate-level education. Possession of a bachelor s degree alongside eligibility under the Superior Academic Achievement (SAA) provision. To qualify for SAA, you must have a bachelor s degree from an accredited institution and meet one of these marks: A GPA of 3.0 or higher on a 4.0 scale for all completed undergraduate courses, or those completed during the final two years of undergraduate study. A GPA of 3.5 or higher on a 4.0 scale for all courses within the major field of study, or those major courses completed during the final two years of study. Class standing in the upper one-third of the graduating class within the college, university, or major subdivision. Election to membership in a national honor society recognized by the Association of College Honor Societies (excluding freshman honor societies). GS-09 Level Candidates must meet at least one of the following criteria: Completion of two full academic years of graduate-level education. Possession of a master s degree or an equivalent graduate degree. Important Accreditation Details All degrees and certificates must be issued by institutions recognized by the U.S. Department of Education. Undergraduate certificates must contain a minimum of 30 semester hours (or 45 quarter hours), and graduate certificates must contain at least 18 semester hours (or 27 quarter hours). The Department of State does not accept certificates credited with Continuing Education Units (CEUs). Education completed in foreign universities is acceptable only if the applicant provides explicit, official evidence at the time of application showing that the foreign education is directly comparable to an accredited U.S. education program. The Evaluation and Assessment Process The federal hiring system utilizes a multi-tiered review process to categorize applicants based on their qualifications: The Crediting Plan Score: Human resources and systems will review your online assessment responses and resume to verify minimum qualifications. Eligible applicants are assigned a score between 70 and 100. Subject Matter Expert (SME) Review: Resumes of top-scoring candidates undergo a rigorous manual review by an SME. The SME evaluates specialized experience directly from the text of the resume. Quality Categories: Applicants are ultimately separated into three distinct tiers: Qualified (Q): Meets minimum requirements and shows basic competency in core knowledge, skills, and abilities (KSAs). Well Qualified (WQ): Shows high proficiency in some, but not all, required competencies and KSAs. Highly Qualified (HQ): Demonstrates complete proficiency in all required competencies and KSAs for the position. Step-by-Step Application Instructions Applications must be submitted fully through the electronic Gateway to State automated system, accessible via USAJOBS. Step 1: Prepare Your Two-Page Resume Your resume is subject to an absolute page limit. Resumes longer than two pages will be deemed completely ineligible and will be excluded from all further consideration. Your resume must include: Full contact information. Completed coursework detailing specific unit/credit hours. Accurate academic standing and cumulative GPA. Complete work and volunteer history, explicitly detailing your weekly work schedule and the exact number of hours worked per week. The formal title, series, and grade of any prior federal positions held. The name, phone number, and email address of your supervisor for each employment period, alongside an explicit statement indicating whether the hiring team has permission to contact them. A list of professional or academic references. Step 2: Gather Academic Transcripts Unofficial transcripts are acceptable for the initial application phase. High school students must submit their most recent report card. Your academic files must clearly show your name, the school s name, your specific degree program, your current/cumulative GPA, and all past and current coursework. Step 3: Provide Proof of Enrollment and Graduation Dates Enrollment: You must upload documentation proving class registration or enrollment for the Fall 2026 semester. If you are graduating in Fall 2026, you must upload an official acceptance letter to a subsequent qualifying program for the Spring 2027 semester to ensure continuous student eligibility. Graduation Proof: Your transcripts or uploaded documentation must explicitly state a projected graduation date that occurs after the Spring 2027 semester. Step 4: Include Veteran Documentation (If Applicable) 5-Point Preference: Submit a legible copy of your DD-214 (Member Copy 4 is preferred) showing honorable discharge and active-duty dates. Active duty members must provide an official branch letterhead certificate confirming service dates and an expected discharge date occurring no later than 120 days after the certificate s submission. 10-Point Preference: Submit a completed Standard Form 15 (SF-15) alongside all official supporting documentation, such as a formal letter from the Department of Veterans Affairs. Step 5: Submit Electronically Log into USAJOBS, click Apply Online to transition to the Gateway to State portal, complete the questionnaire, attach all required files, and finalize your submission before 11:59 p.m. Eastern Time on the closing date. For technical support with the online application portal, candidates can email the Help Desk at mgshelp@monster.com or call (866) 656-6830. Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Job opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post Launch Your Federal Career: U.S. Department of State Pathways Student Trainee Program in Program Analysis appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Singapore AI Safety Fellowship 2026 in Singapore: Fully Funded 3-Month AI Safety Research Fellowship with Monthly Stipend, Housing, Travel Support, and Up to USD 30,000 Research Compute
summary: Artificial Intelligence is transforming every aspect of society, creating extraordinary opportunities while also introducing unprecedented challenges. As AI systems become increasingly powerful, ensuring that they remain safe, secure, trustworthy, and beneficial for humanity has become one of the world s most urgent priorities. To help address this challenge, the Singapore AI Safety Fellowship 2026 is now accepting applications from talented researchers around the world. The fellowship offers an exceptional opportunity for emerging researchers to spend three months in Singapore working alongside internationally recognized experts in AI safety and governance. Participants will receive mentorship, financial support, research funding, professional development, and access to a vibrant international research community dedicated to building safer AI systems. Application Deadline: July 10, 2026 About the Singapore AI Safety Fellowship The Singapore AI Safety Fellowship is an independent research fellowship designed to develop the next generation of AI safety researchers capable of contributing to international cooperation in AI safety research. The fellowship connects participants with leading mentors working on technical AI safety and AI governance while providing opportunities to conduct impactful research that can influence real-world AI policy and practice—not just academic publications. Located in one of the world s leading innovation hubs, Singapore provides an ideal environment where research, policy, government, and international collaboration intersect. The fellowship is advised by Ryan Kidd , Co-Executive Director of the MATS Program. Fellowship Duration Successful applicants will participate in a full-time residential fellowship in Singapore. Program Dates 21 September 2026 4 December 2026 Duration: Three months Participants are expected to relocate to Singapore for the entire fellowship period. Fellowship Benefits The Singapore AI Safety Fellowship provides comprehensive support to ensure participants can focus entirely on their research. Successful fellows will receive: Monthly Stipend Participants receive: SGD 5,000 per month This financial support helps cover living expenses throughout the fellowship. Housing Accommodation is fully provided during the entire fellowship period. Travel Support The fellowship also covers travel arrangements, making it easier for international participants to relocate to Singapore. Research Compute Researchers whose projects require significant computational resources can receive: Up to USD 30,000 in research compute This enables fellows to conduct advanced AI experiments and technical research. Dedicated Mentorship Every fellow receives mentorship from experienced AI safety researchers through regular one-on-one meetings designed to: Improve research quality Provide technical guidance Offer career advice Strengthen publication potential Research Management Support Dedicated Research Managers help fellows: Stay on schedule Overcome research obstacles Improve productivity Plan future academic and professional careers Office Space Participants will work from the SASH headquarters located in the heart of Singapore s historic Chinatown district. The central location provides an inspiring environment for collaboration with fellow researchers and mentors. Community and Networking The fellowship includes: Workshops Research talks Networking sessions Social events Collaboration opportunities Participants become part of an international community working at the intersection of AI safety, governance, research, and public policy. Three Core Pillars of the Fellowship 1. Cross-Regional Collaboration The fellowship brings together researchers from both Eastern and Western regions to encourage: International cooperation Cross-cultural understanding Long-term research partnerships Global AI safety coordination 2. Technical Research Excellence Participants investigate some of today s most pressing AI safety challenges under the guidance of leading researchers. The program emphasizes: High-quality technical research Frontier AI systems AI robustness AI security Trustworthy AI 3. Translating Research into Policy Unlike many research fellowships, this program encourages fellows to ensure their work has practical real-world impact. Participants learn how to: Translate technical findings into policy recommendations Communicate research to policymakers Support responsible AI governance Bridge the gap between research and implementation Distinguished Mentors Participants will work with internationally respected researchers from leading universities and AI organizations. Current mentors include experts affiliated with: Hunter College (CUNY) National University of Singapore (NUS) Tsinghua University Fudan University Hebrew University Oxford Martin AI Governance Initiative Anthropic FAR.AI Concordia AI Neo Research Additional mentors will be announced before the fellowship begins. Who Should Apply? The fellowship seeks talented emerging researchers who are ready to contribute to global AI safety research. Applicants should demonstrate: Strong technical research experience Interest in AI safety and governance Passion for ensuring AI benefits humanity Ability to work across cultures Ability to collaborate across multiple disciplines Willingness to relocate to Singapore for three months Application Process The selection process consists of several stages. Stage 1 Complete the written application. Estimated completion time: Approximately one hour Stage 2 Shortlisted candidates complete standardized assessments. Stage 3 Candidates may be asked to complete: Mentor-specific work assignments Technical interviews Frequently Asked Questions Applicants often ask about: Mentor matching Remote participation Part-time participation Expected research outputs Team projects Housing arrangements Travel support Visa requirements Detailed guidance is available from the fellowship organizers. Information Sessions Prospective applicants can attend free online information sessions available in both English and Mandarin. English Session Date: Wednesday, 1 July Time: 8:00 PM Singapore Time Equivalent Times: 1:00 PM London 8:00 AM New York Mandarin Session Date: Thursday, 2 July Time: 5:00 PM Singapore Time These sessions provide applicants with an opportunity to learn more about the fellowship and ask questions directly. Why You Should Apply The Singapore AI Safety Fellowship is much more than a traditional research fellowship. It provides: Fully supported international research experience Access to world-class AI safety mentors Significant research funding Monthly financial support Free housing Travel assistance International networking opportunities Professional career development Exposure to AI governance and policymaking A unique opportunity to contribute to one of the world s most important technological challenges Researchers interested in shaping the future of safe and trustworthy artificial intelligence are strongly encouraged to apply before the application deadline. Application Deadline: July 10, 2026 Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post Singapore AI Safety Fellowship 2026 in Singapore: Fully Funded 3-Month AI Safety Research Fellowship with Monthly Stipend, Housing, Travel Support, and Up to USD 30,000 Research Compute appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNITAR’s Empowering Youth and Women Programme 2026: A Fully Funded Japan-Africa Capacity Development Opportunity for Sub-Saharan African Innovators
summary: The United Nations Institute for Training and Research (UNITAR), in partnership with the Government and People of Japan, has officially launched one of the most comprehensive and transformative capacity development programmes of 2026 the Empowering Youth and Women amid Economic Instability: Enhancing Public-Private Partnership between Japan and Africa for Sub-Saharan African Countries, Addressing Urgent Challenges, and Promoting TICAD9 programme. This fully funded, multi-phase initiative is designed to equip young and women professionals from Anglophone Sub-Saharan Africa with the entrepreneurial skills, leadership competencies, innovation tools, and international partnership opportunities they need to build scalable, impactful, and investment-ready solutions for their communities and countries. Running from August 2026 to February 2027, this blended programme combines virtual learning, applied project development, expert mentorship, and for a select group of high-performing participants, an immersive in-person experience in Japan. It is implemented under the framework of the Tokyo International Conference on African Development 9 (TICAD9) and represents a bold commitment to strengthening Africa-Japan collaboration in areas including innovation, technology, sustainable industrialization, digital transformation, clean energy, agribusiness, and inclusive economic development. If you are an ambitious young African professional, entrepreneur, researcher, or social innovator between the ages of 18 and 35, this programme represents a once-in-a-generation opportunity to grow your idea into a credible, partnership-ready initiative with real-world impact at zero cost to you. Background: Why This Programme Exists Sub-Saharan Africa is at a pivotal crossroads. The region is experiencing rapid demographic growth, sweeping digital disruption, evolving trade opportunities, mounting climate pressures, persistent food security challenges, and an urgent demand for quality jobs and resilient livelihoods. Young people and women, who make up a significant and growing portion of the continent s population, are increasingly at the forefront of innovation, entrepreneurship, and community-driven solutions. Yet, despite this enormous potential, many promising ideas and initiatives across the continent struggle to move beyond the early stages. The reasons are well-documented: limited access to professional networks, lack of investment readiness, absence of strategic partnerships, and insufficient market linkages continue to hold back a generation of talented individuals who could otherwise drive transformational change. At the same time, there is a growing global interest in deepening practical, mutually beneficial collaboration between African and Japanese innovation ecosystems. Japan s expertise in technology, manufacturing, sustainable industrialization, digital infrastructure, and human-centered economic development aligns powerfully with Africa s development priorities and entrepreneurial energy. It is in this context that UNITAR, supported by the Japanese government, has launched this multi-phase programme to bridge the gap between potential and impact helping African youth and women to transform innovative ideas into scalable, investment-ready, and partnership-oriented initiatives aligned with national and regional development goals. Programme Overview Programme Title: Empowering Youth and Women amid Economic Instability: Enhancing Public-Private Partnership between Japan and Africa for Sub-Saharan African Countries Organizer: United Nations Institute for Training and Research (UNITAR) Funding Partner: Government and People of Japan, Ministry of Foreign Affairs Framework: Tokyo International Conference on African Development 9 (TICAD9) Location: Primarily virtual (online), with a limited in-person component in Hiroshima, Japan for selected participants Duration: 7 months (August 2026 to February 2027) Programme Area: Public Finance and Trade Language: English Mode of Delivery: Blended (virtual and in-person) Cost: Free fully funded by the Government and People of Japan Deadline to Apply: 31 July 2026 Contact: tftp@unitar.org Website: http://www.unitar.org Learning Objectives By the end of the programme, participants will have developed the knowledge, skills, and confidence to: Design innovative, inclusive, and sustainable business or development solutions that are aligned with national and regional priorities Apply digital technologies, artificial intelligence, and emerging innovation tools to address challenges in food security, climate resilience, employment, trade, healthcare, industrialization, and service delivery Develop operationally feasible and financially viable project concepts with clear implementation pathways and partnership strategies Strengthen leadership, negotiation, stakeholder engagement, and cross-sector collaboration competencies Integrate sustainability, gender equality, social impact, and the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) into their entrepreneurial and innovation initiatives Formulate partnership-ready and investment-oriented proposals informed by real market conditions and public-private collaboration principles Communicate project value propositions effectively to investors, development partners, public institutions, and private-sector stakeholders Build long-term professional networks and collaboration opportunities across African and international innovation ecosystems Programme Structure: Four Progressive Phases The programme is built around four carefully sequenced phases that take participants on a journey from foundational learning through applied project development, international engagement, and high-level partnership dialogue. Each phase builds on the previous one, ensuring that participants are continuously deepening their skills and advancing the quality and readiness of their projects. Phase I: Foundations and Opportunity Development (August to October 2026) The first phase lays the groundwork for everything that follows. Participants will receive structured foundational training covering: Entrepreneurship and innovation principles Artificial intelligence and digital transformation Leadership and systems thinking frameworks Sustainable development and SDG integration strategies Market analysis and customer discovery methodologies Business ecosystems and value chain dynamics Introduction to partnerships, investment readiness, and collaborative engagement The focus of this phase is to strengthen participants ability to identify real-world opportunities, understand market and development needs, and begin building solution-oriented concepts with practical relevance and genuine growth potential. Phase II: Applied Innovation and Project Development, Including Training of Trainers (November to December 2026) In the second phase, selected participants will move into deeper, more practical application of the skills they have developed. This phase includes: Business model refinement and optimization Financial and operational planning Project structuring and implementation design Monitoring, evaluation, and impact measurement frameworks Pitch preparation and professional communication Partnership mapping and stakeholder engagement strategies Training-of-Trainers (ToT) components to support local knowledge multiplication By the end of this phase, participants will have progressively transformed their ideas into implementation-oriented and partnership-ready project concepts that are scalable, credible, and capable of attracting collaboration and operational support. Phase III: Leadership and International Engagement, Including Japan Immersion (January to February 2027) A limited number of high-performing participants will be selected to participate in an advanced hybrid engagement phase that includes an exclusive short immersion programme in Japan.
