Activities during this phase may include: One-on-one and group mentorship sessions with industry and ecosystem experts Exposure to Japan s world-class innovation and entrepreneurship ecosystems Learning visits to leading institutions and enterprises Institutional dialogues and strategic networking opportunities Project refinement workshops and partnership discussions with potential collaborators and investors This phase is highly competitive. Selection for the Japan immersion component will be based on implementation potential, practical feasibility, partnership readiness, and long-term impact potential. Not all programme participants will be selected for this in-person component, and all selection decisions made by UNITAR and its partners are final. Phase IV: Africa-Japan Innovation and Partnership Round Table (February 2027) The programme concludes with a high-level dialogue and engagement platform bringing together participants, ecosystem actors, development partners, mentors, investors, and representatives from both the public and private sectors across Africa and Japan. Selected participants will have the opportunity to: Showcase their initiatives to a diverse audience of stakeholders Exchange lessons learned and best practices with peers and experts Explore practical collaboration pathways for implementation and scaling of their projects Establish long-term strategic connections with investors, development partners, and ecosystem institutions Teaching Methodology The programme applies UNITAR s proven adult learning and instructional design methodologies, combining multiple approaches to maximize practical impact: Competency-based and results-oriented learning Project-based and experiential methodologies Practical application and real-world problem solving Peer learning and collaborative group engagement Expert mentorship and continuous guidance Continuous feedback and evidence-based evaluation Ecosystem exposure and structured partnership facilitation This multi-layered approach ensures that participants are not just absorbing theoretical knowledge but actively applying what they learn to real challenges and real projects with tangible outcomes. Target Sectors Participants working in or aspiring to contribute to the following sectors are particularly encouraged to apply: Agri-food Innovation and Bioeconomy Artificial Intelligence, Robotics, and Smart Digital Solutions Fintech and Financial Inclusion Clean Technologies and Strategic Resources Climate and Environmental Solutions Healthcare and MedTech Sustainable Cities and Mobility Blue Economy Creative Industries Light Manufacturing and Industrial Solutions Skills Development and the Future of Work Social Impact and Essential Services Selection Criteria The programme seeks motivated individuals with strong potential to translate ideas into practical, measurable impact. Particular consideration will be given to applicants who demonstrate: Innovation and problem-solving capacity Leadership potential and commitment to community or economic development Clear project or business ideas with real implementation potential Openness to collaboration and partnership development Commitment to learning, mentorship, and long-term programme engagement Potential for scalability, sustainability, and measurable impact Due to the high volume of applications expected, UNITAR and its partners will conduct a careful review of all submissions and select only the candidates with the strongest profiles and most compelling applications. Progression to subsequent phases of the programme is competitive and based on performance, engagement, leadership, and the quality and feasibility of the proposed project or initiative. Certification and Costs Certificates of completion will be awarded to participants who successfully complete each phase of the programme Participation is entirely free, funded by the Government and People of Japan Selected participants invited to the Japan immersion phase (Phase III) may be responsible for limited personal expenses, but the core programme costs are covered Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the UNITAR Empowering Youth and Women Programme 2026 is 31 July 2026 . Prospective applicants are strongly encouraged to submit their applications well before the deadline to allow sufficient time for review and processing. APPLY HERE For inquiries, contact: tftp@unitar.org Visit HERE for more information about the UNITAR s Empowering Youth and Women Programme 2026. Conclusion The UNITAR Empowering Youth and Women amid Economic Instability Programme 2026 is far more than a training course it is a comprehensive, structured, and internationally connected capacity development journey that can genuinely transform the trajectory of a young African professional s career and the communities they serve. With zero cost to participants, access to world-class mentorship, a potential immersion experience in Japan, and a high-level platform to showcase finished projects to investors and development partners, the value of this programme is exceptional by any standard. Whether you are an aspiring entrepreneur with an idea still taking shape, a social innovator already building solutions on the ground, or a professional looking to scale your impact, this programme has been designed with you in mind. The application deadline of 31 July 2026 is firm, and spaces are limited so if you are between 18 and 35, based in an eligible Anglophone Sub-Saharan African country, and driven by a desire to create lasting economic and social change, there is no reason to wait. Apply today and take the first step toward turning your vision into a reality on the global stage. Discover more global Fellowship and UN opportunities on OFY. The post UNITAR s Empowering Youth and Women Programme 2026: A Fully Funded Japan-Africa Capacity Development Opportunity for Sub-Saharan African Innovators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: World Health Summit 2026 Youth Group Opportunity: How Your Organization Can Attend the World’s Premier Global Health Event for Free in Berlin
summary: The World Health Summit (WHS) 2026 has officially opened applications for one of the most meaningful and accessible opportunities available to young health advocates around the world complimentary group tickets for youth-led organizations to attend the prestigious World Health Summit 2026 in Berlin, Germany. If your organization works at the intersection of health, advocacy, youth empowerment, education, or social impact, this is your chance to place your team at the very center of global health dialogue, entirely free of charge. This article provides a complete and thorough breakdown of the WHS 2026 Youth Group Opportunity what it is, what it includes, who qualifies, how to apply, and everything else you need to know to submit a strong application before the deadline. What Is the World Health Summit? The World Health Summit is one of the world s most influential and high-profile global health conferences, bringing together heads of state, health ministers, leading scientists, researchers, public health experts, civil society organizations, private sector representatives, and young health advocates from across the globe. Held annually in Berlin, Germany, the summit serves as a platform for urgent dialogue on the most pressing health challenges facing humanity from pandemic preparedness and universal health coverage to climate and health, digital health innovation, mental health, and health equity. The World Health Summit is deeply committed to the principle that meaningful change in global health requires the active participation of young people not as passive observers, but as engaged contributors, innovators, and advocates with real perspectives and lived experiences that must inform global decision-making. It is out of this commitment that the WHS 2026 Youth Group Opportunity has been created. About the WHS 2026 Youth Group Opportunity The WHS 2026 Youth Group Opportunity is a structured initiative through which the World Health Summit provides a number of complimentary tickets to youth-led organizations and groups, enabling them to attend all three days of the summit in Berlin at no cost. The initiative directly reflects the summit s core values of fostering diversity, inclusivity, and intergenerational dialogue in global health. Rather than limiting youth participation to individual ticket holders, the programme is deliberately structured around organizations and groups recognizing that collective youth voices carry greater weight, and that meaningful participation in global health conversations is most powerful when it is rooted in organizational context and community impact. Event: World Health Summit 2026 (WHS 2026) Location: Berlin, Germany Format: In-person conference Duration: Three days Cost for selected youth organizations: Free (complimentary tickets) Application Deadline: 31 July 2026 Application Method: Official online application form What Is Included in the Complimentary Tickets Selected youth-led organizations will receive complimentary access that covers the full scope of the WHS 2026 conference experience. Here is what is included: Full access to all three days of the World Health Summit 2026 in Berlin The ability to contribute perspectives, experiences, and ideas to global health discussions taking place at the summit Engagement with global health leaders, experts, scientists, policymakers, and fellow youth advocates through exclusive networking opportunities Access to coffee breaks and lunches provided during the summit days Access to social evening events that form part of the WHS 2026 programme It is essential to note, however, that the following are not covered and must be arranged and funded independently by participating organizations and their nominated members: International or domestic travel to and from Berlin Accommodation in Berlin during the summit Visa application fees and related documentation costs Any other personal or travel-related expenses Additionally, the World Health Summit is not in a position to provide direct support or assistance with individual visa applications. However, upon completing registration for WHS 2026, participants will receive an automated registration confirmation email, which may be used as supporting documentation when applying for a visa independently. Who Can Apply: Eligibility Criteria The WHS 2026 Youth Group Opportunity is designed specifically for organizations, not individuals. Applications from individuals will not be considered under any circumstances. The following eligibility criteria apply: The applicant must be a youth-led organization, network, or group The organization must primarily represent young people The organization must demonstrate active engagement within its community The organization must be working in one or more of the following fields: health, youth empowerment, education, advocacy, social impact, or related areas All nominated participants must be 28 years of age or younger at the time of WHS 2026 A minimum of 3 and a maximum of 15 complimentary tickets may be requested per organization The World Health Summit particularly welcomes and encourages applications from diverse groups and organizations, including those representing: Underrepresented geographic regions and countries Marginalized populations and communities A broad range of social, cultural, and professional backgrounds Diversity considerations will be formally and meaningfully taken into account during the selection process, reflecting the summit s deep commitment to equity and inclusive global health participation. How to Apply: Step-by-Step Guide The application process for the WHS 2026 Youth Group Opportunity is straightforward but requires thoughtful preparation. Here is what you need to do: Step 1: Read All Programme Information Before beginning your application, download the official WHS 2026 Youth Group Opportunity document from the World Health Summit website. This document contains all the relevant information and guidance for applicants. Step 2: Prepare Your Application Content Your application must comprehensively address the following four components: Your organization s focus and field of work describe clearly what your organization does, who it serves, and how it operates Your motivation for participating in the World Health Summit 2026 articulate specifically why attending WHS 2026 matters to your organization and your community An example of your organization s work and its impact provide a concrete, real-world example that demonstrates what your organization has achieved and the difference it has made The topics you would like to engage with at WHS 2026 and how you will share your experience explain which global health topics are most relevant to your work, and describe how your team plans to bring the knowledge and connections gained at the summit back to your community Step 3: Complete the Online Application Form Submit your completed application through the official online application form available on the World Health Summit website. The deadline for submission is 31 July 2026. Applications submitted after this date or incomplete applications will not be considered. How Applications Are Evaluated All applications received by the deadline will be evaluated based on two primary factors: Completeness of the application all required fields and components must be fully addressed Quality of the content the clarity, depth, and impact of the information provided across all four application components In the event that the number of qualifying applications exceeds the number of available complimentary tickets, the World Health Summit will employ a lottery system to determine which organizations are selected as recipients. This approach ensures that the process remains fair and transparent even when demand outpaces supply. Why This Opportunity Matters for Youth-Led Organizations Global health decisions made at forums like the World Health Summit have real and lasting consequences for communities around the world. Young people and the organizations that represent them have an indispensable role to play in shaping those decisions but access to high-level global health conferences has historically been a significant barrier for youth-led organizations, particularly those from lower-income countries or underrepresented regions. The WHS 2026 Youth Group Opportunity directly addresses this barrier by removing the cost of conference attendance and ensuring that a diverse range of youth voices is present in the room where these conversations happen. For any youth-led organization working in health, advocacy, or social impact, the value of being present at the World Health Summit extends far beyond the three days of the event itself. The connections made, the knowledge gained, the visibility earned, and the relationships built at WHS 2026 can have lasting effects on the trajectory of an organization and the communities it serves. Visit HERE for more information about the World Health Summit 2026 Youth Group Opportunity. Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the WHS 2026 Youth Group Opportunity is 31 July 2026 . Given that applications are evaluated on quality and that a lottery may be used if demand exceeds supply, organizations are strongly encouraged to begin preparing their applications early and to submit well before the deadline. For programme details, download the WHS 2026 Youth Group Opportunity document directly from the World Health Summit website. Click HERE to download the program information. APPLY NOW Conclusion The World Health Summit 2026 Youth Group Opportunity is a rare and genuinely valuable initiative that removes one of the most significant barriers to youth participation in global health: the cost of access. By offering complimentary tickets to youth-led organizations from around the world, the WHS is making a concrete and meaningful statement about the role that young people must play in shaping the future of health globally. For any youth-led organization working in health, advocacy, education, youth empowerment, or social impact whether you are based in a major city or a remote community, whether your team is large or small, whether you work locally, nationally, or regionally this is an opportunity to bring your organization to the global stage, to engage with the world s leading health minds, and to return home with the knowledge, networks, and inspiration needed to deepen your impact. The application process is accessible and the deadline of 31 July 2026 is fast approaching. Do not wait gather your team, prepare a compelling application, and take your organization to Berlin.
